entries.title: Theming a generated-image system with CSS design tokens
entries.summary: Ghostwriter's post images moved from a dark look to a light one by flipping a single CSS class. Here is how to build a themeable system on design tokens, render it to a deterministic PNG, and the one token I forgot that made my text disappear.

entries.title: A new card type, and the case for growing a vocabulary over options
entries.summary: Ghostwriter got a matrix comparison card. The more interesting part is the rule behind it: when a post shape recurs it earns its own card type, instead of collapsing everything into one configurable mega-card.

entries.title: Cohesion is leverage: pulling a drifting card family onto one base
entries.summary: Ghostwriter's image cards had drifted into separate looks. Redesigning them onto one shared base isn't just about brand; the base is what makes the next card type cheap to add and structurally unable to drift. Here's the full build, from tokens to a rendered PNG to the check that keeps the family honest.

entries.title: Reach is earned in dwell time: encoding LinkedIn's feed rules into a posting tool
entries.summary: ghostwriter v0.3.0 turned a pile of researched LinkedIn reach rules into config the skill reads on every draft, and added native carousels by rendering HTML slides into a PDF that LinkedIn shows as a swipeable document. Here is the full build: the playbook as data, the slide-to-PDF render, the documents-API upload, and the check that runs before anything publishes.

entries.title: An attribution mark the common crops can't remove: mark every region a reshare keeps
entries.summary: The LinkedIn ghostwriter got new card templates, but the real lesson was attribution: a corner byline crops off and a tiled watermark looks awful, so the fix was to put a mark on every region a reshare keeps, bracketing the headline with the signature and co-locating the handle with the figure, so a bottom-strip, top-strip, or single-side crop can't take a region without leaving a mark. Here is the full build from card template to rendered PNG.

entries.title: A skill is code: scoring the SKILL.md and testing the glue around it
entries.summary: A Claude skill is software, so I held the ghostwriter skill to software's bar: a scorer that structurally grades the SKILL.md as a CI gate, 100% line coverage on its glue scripts by mocking the network and browser boundaries, and an honest line about what coverage doesn't prove.

entries.title: You already have an image renderer: HTML and Mermaid into PNGs on your own machine
entries.summary: Ghostwriter can now attach a rendered diagram or card to a post, and it renders entirely through a local headless browser instead of a third-party service. The two lessons worth keeping are that a browser you already run is a capable image renderer, and that personal styling belongs in config that never touches the shared repo.
